title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Morningside Heights | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Morningside Heights, Manhattan. Safe, humane bat control for homes and buildings. Call Best Pest Control NYC today!
intro: Morningside Heights' historic buildings and proximity to Morningside Park create ideal conditions for bat infestations in attics, chimneys, and wall voids. Best Pest Control NYC provides safe, humane bat removal and exclusion services tailored to the unique architecture of this Upper Manhattan neighborhood. Our certified technicians ensure bats are removed legally and your property is sealed against future intrusions.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has over 15 years of experience serving Morningside Heights residents, from brownstones near Columbia University to pre-war apartment buildings along Riverside Drive. We use humane, NYS-compliant exclusion methods and offer thorough inspections, cleanup, and long-term prevention guarantees.
localContext: Morningside Heights' century-old buildings, including those near Columbia University, St. John the Divine Cathedral, and Riverside Church, often feature architectural gaps that attract roosting bats. The neighborhood's tree-lined streets and proximity to Morningside and Riverside Parks make it particularly vulnerable to seasonal bat activity from spring through fall.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Morningside Heights?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Morningside Heights typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and extent of exclusion work needed. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before any work begins.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in New York City?
faqs.answer: Yes, but it must be done humanely and in compliance with New York State regulations. Bats are protected species and cannot be harmed. Our licensed technicians use exclusion methods that allow bats to leave safely while preventing re-entry, especially important during maternity season from May through August.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my Morningside Heights building?
faqs.answer: Common signs include scratching or squeaking sounds at dusk or dawn, droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, grease marks around gaps in siding or rooflines, and bats flying around your building at sunset. Older buildings in Morningside Heights are particularly susceptible due to architectural gaps and chimney access.

faqs.question: How long does the bat exclusion process take?
faqs.answer: The complete bat exclusion process typically takes 2-4 weeks. After the initial inspection, we install one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to exit but not return. Once we confirm all bats have left, we seal all entry points and perform sanitization if needed. Timeline varies based on colony size and building structure.
